title: UPI's next hundred million users live in small-town India
dek: Transaction data shows the fastest growth in payments is no longer in the metros. Tier-3 towns and rural districts are driving the curve.

author.name: Ishita Rao
author.bio: Ishita covers society, consumption and technology adoption across urban and rural India.

body.text: For years the story of digital payments in India was a metro story: QR codes at Bengaluru coffee shops, autos in Mumbai that stopped carrying change. The latest transaction data suggests that chapter is over.

Growth in per-capita transaction counts is now fastest in districts classified as semi-urban and rural. Kirana stores, agricultural mandis and small-town services are the new frontier — and average ticket sizes there are falling, a sign that everyday purchases, not just large transfers, have moved on-rail.
The most important payments chart in India is no longer about whether the line goes up. It is about where the line goes up.
